acceptance_targets: [CH16_CRAFT_DAYCOUNT_CONTINUITY] Pass if a cold reader can read snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:11 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:45; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:47; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:51 and get one consistent answer about how many days have elapsed since the Day 2 dead-man's-switch announcement. Fail if the revised passage still says or implies twelve elapsed days while also calling the present scene Day 13.

[CH16_CRAFT_SANITIZED_BLANKET_KILLING] Pass if, by reading the revised material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:67; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:69; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:75; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:87; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:93, a cold reader can state what the extinguisher does to Rafiq's body and what Noor hears or sees that makes the violence physically ugly in real time. Fail if the killing still depends mainly on placeholder thumps plus retrospective summary.

[CH16_AWARD_LATE_PAGE_THEMATIC_REPROOF] Pass if the revised closing page still shows individualized Ember outreach and Jae-won's fixation on the balance bike, but reaches its meaning once: after the first clear arrival in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:207 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:233, no later sentence restates in abstract terms that Ember is politely indifferent or that survival is the chapter's true accomplishment. The ending must finish on a concrete observable detail or change in pressure, not on a second thematic paraphrase.
[F25] Aftermath discovery includes at minimum the weapon's specific damage characteristics.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can read snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:11 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:45; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:47; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:51 and get one consistent answer about how many days have elapsed since the Day 2 dead-man's-switch announcement. Fail if the revised passage still says or implies twelve elapsed days while also calling the present scene Day 13.
findings.problem: The chapter's own time math slips. Noor explicitly anchors the scene on Day 13 at line 47, but line 11 says the dead man's switch was announced "twelve days ago." The continuity sheet places that announcement on Day 2, so at Day 13 sundown the elapsed time is eleven days, not twelve. That is a canon timeline contradiction, not just a fuzzy impression of duration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:11 so its elapsed-time phrasing agrees with the Day 13 anchor at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:47. If the intent is actually a different current day, then adjust the local day-count exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:45; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:47; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:51 instead of leaving both markers in conflict.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by reading the revised material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:67; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:69; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:75; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:87; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:93, a cold reader can state what the extinguisher does to Rafiq's body and what Noor hears or sees that makes the violence physically ugly in real time. Fail if the killing still depends mainly on placeholder thumps plus retrospective summary.
findings.problem: The chapter's main new horror beat retreats at the moment of first impact. Noor hears vague thumps in the dark, then arrives to a forensic aftermath and retrospective explanation that Rafiq was probably already dead before the last blow. In a horror novel whose constitution and risk policy explicitly reject cut-away violence, this reads as sanitization drift: the ugly physicality of the fire-extinguisher killing is inferred after the fact instead of being made fully present on the page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the corridor-death sequence across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:67; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:69; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:75; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:87; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:93 so the beat stays entropic and dark without hiding behind generic impact noise. Options are to get Noor downstairs in time to witness one or two decisive blows, or to make the heard impacts and immediate post-kill traces specific enough that the reader experiences the extinguisher attack before the corpse inventory begins.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised closing page still shows individualized Ember outreach and Jae-won's fixation on the balance bike, but reaches its meaning once: after the first clear arrival in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:207 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:233, no later sentence restates in abstract terms that Ember is politely indifferent or that survival is the chapter's true accomplishment. The ending must finish on a concrete observable detail or change in pressure, not on a second thematic paraphrase.
findings.problem: Once the chapter has already landed its state shift and Priya's waiting posture, the final page keeps translating the same point into cleaner thematic language: Ember's polite persistence, survival as the only accomplishment, silence as Ember's silence, the building holding its breath. That is overstatement after arrival and mild chapter sprawl. Instead of buying one more concrete turn, the ending re-proves what the chapter has already made legible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and rebuild the closing pass at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:207; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:211; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:213; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:223; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:229; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:233. The revision still needs to preserve three must-land beats: Ember is messaging each survivor separately, Jae-won is dissociatively fixed on Seo-yeon's bike, and the chapter ends with four people waiting for morning. After the first clear arrival, stop paraphrasing what those facts mean and spend the remaining lines on one new observable pressure, image, or consequence.

findings.acceptance_test: Aftermath discovery includes at minimum the weapon's specific damage characteristics.
findings.evidence: Lines 3155-3198: Noor finds Dane already wounded from Rafiq's attack.
findings.problem: Dane Olerud's killing happens off-page. Noor arrives to find him already wounded. The aftermath is rendered with acceptable specificity (screwdriver wound, bleeding, hand closing around nothing) but the act itself is absent.
findings.rewrite_direction: If Noor cannot witness the fight, ensure the body discovery renders the specific wound mechanics (entry angle, depth, what the screwdriver displaced).

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 16 contains em-dashes on no more than 7 prose lines (≈8%).
findings.problem: Chapter 16 has 34.7% em-dash density (33 of 95 prose lines contain em-dashes), far above the 10% threshold. Manuscript-wide density is 43.2%.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines to ~7 (≈8% of 95 prose lines). Convert most parenthetical asides to commas or separate sentences. Reserve em-dashes for the strongest interjections or corrections.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 16 opens no more than 22 sentences with 'She' (≈7%).
findings.problem: Chapter 16 opens 12.7% of sentences with 'She' (40 of 315), contributing to the manuscript-wide 11.6% 'She'-opener rate (threshold: 7%).
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'She'-openers to ~22 (≈7%). Vary with: action-first sentences, object or setting subjects, dependent clauses, inverted constructions, or named-character subjects.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 16 contains at most 5 uses of 'something.'
findings.evidence: 'Something' count: 25 in 4368 words = 1 per 174 words. Manuscript-wide: 252 in 79,023 = 1 per 314 (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: Chapter 16 uses 'something' 25 times in 4368 words (1 per 174), contributing to the manuscript-wide overuse of 252 total. Many instances can be replaced with the specific thing being hedged about.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'something' in Chapter 16 to ~5 instances (≈1 per 800 words). Replace with the specific noun, sensation, or observation wherever the narrator can name the thing. Keep 'something' only where genuine uncertainty is the point.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
